title: Lifecycle, Storage Classes, Archive Restore, and Cost · Program Defense
summary: A production review compares near-miss decisions for lifecycle, storage classes, archive restore, and cost and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for lifecycle and storage-cost decision table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select storage class from access frequency, object size, retrieval latency, resilience, minimum duration, monitoring, and transition cost.
Model filters, current and noncurrent transitions, expiration, delete markers, multipart cleanup, precedence, propagation, and unsupported bucket features.
Request, monitor, authorize, and communicate temporary archive restoration with tier, expiry, duplicate request, and failure states.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Cost Model Changed Traffic
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 3600 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to evaluate lifecycle transition, retention lock, restore, and minimum-duration cost; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Calculate storage, requests, transitions, retrieval, early deletion, transfer, CDN, invalidation, logs, and operational labor under changed traffic.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Cost Model Changed Traffic.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for calculate storage, requests, transitions, retrieval, early deletion, transfer, cdn, invalidation, logs, and operational labor under changed traffic. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Cost Model Changed Traffic, keep Cost Model Changed Traffic connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Calculate storage, requests, transitions, retrieval, early deletion, transfer, CDN, invalidation, logs, and operational labor under changed traffic.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Calculate storage, requests, transitions, retrieval, early deletion, transfer, CDN, invalidation, logs, and operational labor under changed traffic.
